revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|text=この記事の項目名のうち、名の「はれなが」にあたる部分には以下のような表記揺れがあります。 |表記1=•晴栄（新字体） |表記2=•晴榮（旧字体） |表記3=•晴榮（新旧両字体の混用） }} {{統合文字|晴|晴（「日」に「靑」）}} {{政治家 |人名 = 土御門 晴榮 |各国語表記 = つちみかど はれなが |画像 = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg |画像サイズ = 180px |画像説明 = 1913年（大正2年） |国略称 = {{JPN}} |生年月日 = [[1859年]][[6月30日]] |出生地 = |没年月日 = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1859|6|30|1915|10|16}} |死没地 = |出身校 = |前職 = 大学御用掛 |現職 = |所属政党 = |称号・勲章 = 贈[[正三位]]
[[子爵]] |親族（政治家） = [[土御門晴善]]（養孫） |配偶者 = |サイン = |ウェブサイト = |サイトタイトル = |国旗 = JPN |職名 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員 |内閣 = |選挙区 = [[貴族院子爵議員]] |当選回数 = 3回 |就任日 = 1900年6月21日 - 1904年7月9日
1908年3月18日 - 1911年7月9日
1911年7月10日 |退任日 = 1915年10月16日 |所属委員会 = |議員会館 = |元首職 = |元首 = |国旗2 = |職名2 = |内閣2 = |選挙区2 = |当選回数2 = |就任日2 = |退任日2 = |元首職2 = |元首2 = }} '''土御門 晴栄'''（つちみかど はれなが、旧字体：'''晴榮'''、[[1859年]][[6月30日]]（[[安政]]6年[[6月1日 (旧暦)|6月1日]]）{{Sfn|霞会館|1996|p=105}} - [[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）[[10月16日]]）は、[[日本]]の[[陰陽師]]、[[政治家]]。贈[[正三位]]。[[爵位]]は[[子爵]]。[[幼名]]は'''和丸'''。[[旧姓]]は'''錦織'''（にしごり）。名前の読みは「はれよし」とも。 天文暦道御用掛、大学御用掛、[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員（3期）などを歴任した。 == 概要 == [[天文道|天文]]・[[暦道]]・[[陰陽道]]を家業とする[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]の当主である。[[安倍晴明]]など著名な[[陰陽師]]を輩出した[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]の[[嫡流]]であるが、[[家督]]を継いでからは苦難が続いた。養父の急逝により幼くして当主となったが、[[明治]]に入ると[[陰陽寮]]が廃止され、編暦に関する権限、土御門家伝来の特権を次々と失うことになった。晩年は[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員を3期務めて[[帝国議会]]において活動した。 == 来歴 == === 生い立ち === [[公家]]の[[錦織久隆]]の次男として生まれた[[霞会館|霞会館諸家資料調査委員会]]編纂『昭和新修華族家系大成』下巻、[[霞会館]]、1984年、100頁。。[[安土桃山時代]]の土御門家当主・[[土御門久脩|久脩]]から女系を1回経た末裔、また[[江戸時代]]中期の当主・[[土御門泰福|泰福]]から女系を2回経た末裔にあたる{{efn|[[土御門久脩]]-倉橋泰吉-泰房-泰貞-泰章（母は[[土御門泰福]]の娘）-萩原員領室-従言-錦織久雄-久隆-土御門晴榮　''（参照：[[土御門久脩#末裔]]）''}}。[[錦織家]]は[[半家 (公家)|半家]]の[[家格]]であり、久隆は[[刑部省|刑部卿]]などを務めていた。また久隆は晴栄が生まれる前年に起こった[[廷臣八十八卿列参事件]]にかかわっていたことでも知られている。その後、公家の[[土御門晴雄]]の三女と結婚して婿養子に入る。土御門家も半家の家格であり、天文・暦道・陰陽道を家業としていた。晴雄は[[陰陽頭]]や[[民部卿]]などを務めていたが、[[明治維新]]を機に[[江戸幕府|幕府]]の[[天文方]]を廃止するよう主張し、[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]より許された。これにより、天文方の暦算や[[頒暦]]の権限は朝廷に返されることになり、朝廷で[[陰陽寮]]を統括していた土御門家が日本の編暦を一手に担うことになっていった。 === 陰陽師として === 1869年（明治2年）、養父・土御門晴雄が死去したことに伴い「土御門和丸ヨリ父晴雄死去届」『公文録』54巻。、晴栄が土御門家の家督を継いだ「土御門和丸ヨリ天文暦道ノ儀ニ付願」『公文録』55巻。。その翌年には[[天社禁止令]]により陰陽師の免許が禁止され、陰陽寮が廃止されることになったため、陰陽道を家業としていた土御門家にとっては苦難の時代が始まった。 陰陽寮が所管していた天文暦道は、[[大学校 (1869年)|大学校]]（[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]の前身）の下に設置された天文暦道局に移管されることになったが、その際に土御門家の当主として晴栄は新政府より天文暦道御用掛を命じられた内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』16巻、内閣記録局、1889年、303頁。。しかし天文暦道局の本局が東京に移転されることになったため、京都の土御門家はその出先機関のような扱いとなり、出張所が設置されることになった「天文暦道局ヲ東京ニ移シ西京ハ土御門家ヲ以テ官庁ニ充ツ」『太政類典』118巻。。その後天文暦道局が星学局に改組されるにあたり、京都星学局出張所は廃止されることになり内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』2巻、内閣記録局、1889年、19頁。、晴栄も大学御用掛を解任されて内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』2巻、内閣記録局、1889年、20頁。「星学局出張所廃シニ付土御門和丸大学御用掛ヲ免セラル」『太政類典』117巻。、ここに土御門家は編暦に関する権限をすべて失うことになった。 === 政治家として === 明治維新とともに[[堂上家|堂上公家]]として[[華族]]に列していた晴栄は、1884年（明治17年）7月8日の[[華族令]]に基づき[[子爵]]に叙された[[小田部雄次]]『華族』[[中央公論新社]]、2006年、333頁。。その後1900年（明治33年）6月の貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙で当選して「帝國議會」『[[官報]]』5090號、[[印刷局]]、1900年6月22日、313面。同年6月21日から[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員を務め『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、10頁。、1904年（明治37年）7月9日の任期満了まで在任{{efn|『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』48頁では任期満了前の6月に辞任としているが、帝国議会会議録では任期まで在任している。}}。1908年（明治41年）に行われた子爵議員の補選で再び当選、同年3月18日から貴族院議員を務め『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、17頁。、次の1911年（明治44年）7月の第4回子爵議員選挙でも再選されたが『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、20頁。、1915年（大正4年）10月16日議員在職のまま死去『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、23頁。、同日付で[[正三位]]に叙された「叙任及辭令」『官報』965號、印刷局、1915年10月19日、411面。。 晴栄は晩年に子爵[[冷泉為柔]]の長女を後妻に迎えた。また、子爵[[松平信正 (丹波亀山藩主)|松平信正]]の次男・[[土御門晴行|晴行]]を自身の長女と結婚させて婿養子に迎えた。土御門家の家督と爵位はこの晴行が継いでいる。 == 略年譜 == * 1859年（安政6年）- 誕生 * 1870年（明治3年）- 天文暦道御用掛 * 1900年（明治33年）- 貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙当選 * 1908年（明治37年）- 貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙当選 * 1911年（明治44年）- 第四回貴族院子爵議員選挙当選 * 1915年（大正4年）- 死去 == 栄典 == * 1884年（明治17年）叙子爵 * [[1894年]]（明治27年）[[6月30日]] - [[従四位]]『官報』第3301号「叙任及辞令」1894年7月2日。 * 1915年（大正4年）- 贈正三位 == 家族・親族 == * [[土御門晴親]]（養祖父）- [[公家]]、[[陰陽師]] * [[錦織久隆]]（父）- 公家 * [[土御門晴雄]]（養父）- 公家、陰陽師 * [[冷泉為柔]]（岳父）- 公家、[[宮内省|宮内官僚]] * [[土御門藤子]]（養叔母）- 御所女中 * [[土御門晴善]]（養孫）- [[農学者]]、政治家 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、貴族院事務局、1947年。 *衆議院・参議院編『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *{{Citation|和書|editor=霞会館|editor-link=霞会館|title=平成新修旧華族家系大成|volume-title=下巻|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1996}} {{Start box}} {{S-reg|jp}} {{Succession box | title = 子爵 | years = [[土御門家]]初代
[[1884年]] - [[1915年]] | before = （叙爵） | after = [[土御門晴行]] }} {{S-other}} {{Succession box | title = [[土御門家|土御門家当主]] | years = [[1869年]] - 1915年 | before = [[土御門晴雄]] | after = 土御門晴行 }} {{End box}} {{Japan-politician-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:つちみかと はれなか}} [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)|はれなか]] [[Category:貴族院子爵議員]] [[Category:明治時代の貴族院議員]] [[Category:大正時代の貴族院議員]] [[Category:在職中に死去した日本の貴族院議員]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:錦織家]] [[Category:山城国の人物]] [[Category:京都市出身の人物]] [[Category:正三位受位者]] [[Category:1859年生]] [[Category:1915年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[土御門晴榮]] to [[Tsuchimikado Harenaga]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{表記揺れ案内|text=この記事の項目名のうち、名の「はれなが」にあたる部分には以下のような表記揺れがあります。 |表記1=•晴栄（新字体） |表記2=•晴榮（旧字体） |表記3=•晴榮（新旧両字体の混用） }} {{統合文字|晴|晴（「日」に「靑」）}} {{政治家 |人名 = 土御門 晴榮 |各国語表記 = つちみかど はれなが |画像 = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg |画像サイズ = 180px |画像説明 = 1913年（大正2年） |国略称 = {{JPN}} |生年月日 = [[1859年]][[6月30日]] |出生地 = |没年月日 = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1859|6|30|1915|10|16}} |死没地 = |出身校 = |前職 = 大学御用掛 |現職 = |所属政党 = |称号・勲章 = 贈[[正三位]]
[[子爵]] |親族（政治家） = [[土御門晴善]]（養孫） |配偶者 = |サイン = |ウェブサイト = |サイトタイトル = |国旗 = JPN |職名 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員 |内閣 = |選挙区 = [[貴族院子爵議員]] |当選回数 = 3回 |就任日 = 1900年6月21日 - 1904年7月9日
1908年3月18日 - 1911年7月9日
1911年7月10日 |退任日 = 1915年10月16日 |所属委員会 = |議員会館 = |元首職 = |元首 = |国旗2 = |職名2 = |内閣2 = |選挙区2 = |当選回数2 = |就任日2 = |退任日2 = |元首職2 = |元首2 = }} '''土御門 晴栄'''（つちみかど はれなが、旧字体：'''晴榮'''、[[1859年]][[6月30日]]（[[安政]]6年[[6月1日 (旧暦)|6月1日]]）{{Sfn|霞会館|1996|p=105}} - [[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）[[10月16日]]）は、[[日本]]の[[陰陽師]]、[[政治家]]。贈[[正三位]]。[[爵位]]は[[子爵]]。[[幼名]]は'''和丸'''。[[旧姓]]は'''錦織'''（にしごり）。名前の読みは「はれよし」とも。 天文暦道御用掛、大学御用掛、[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員（3期）などを歴任した。 == 概要 == [[天文道|天文]]・[[暦道]]・[[陰陽道]]を家業とする[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]の当主である。[[安倍晴明]]など著名な[[陰陽師]]を輩出した[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]の[[嫡流]]であるが、[[家督]]を継いでからは苦難が続いた。養父の急逝により幼くして当主となったが、[[明治]]に入ると[[陰陽寮]]が廃止され、編暦に関する権限、土御門家伝来の特権を次々と失うことになった。晩年は[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員を3期務めて[[帝国議会]]において活動した。 == 来歴 == === 生い立ち === [[公家]]の[[錦織久隆]]の次男として生まれた[[霞会館|霞会館諸家資料調査委員会]]編纂『昭和新修華族家系大成』下巻、[[霞会館]]、1984年、100頁。。[[安土桃山時代]]の土御門家当主・[[土御門久脩|久脩]]から女系を1回経た末裔、また[[江戸時代]]中期の当主・[[土御門泰福|泰福]]から女系を2回経た末裔にあたる{{efn|[[土御門久脩]]-倉橋泰吉-泰房-泰貞-泰章（母は[[土御門泰福]]の娘）-萩原員領室-従言-錦織久雄-久隆-土御門晴榮　''（参照：[[土御門久脩#末裔]]）''}}。[[錦織家]]は[[半家 (公家)|半家]]の[[家格]]であり、久隆は[[刑部省|刑部卿]]などを務めていた。また久隆は晴栄が生まれる前年に起こった[[廷臣八十八卿列参事件]]にかかわっていたことでも知られている。その後、公家の[[土御門晴雄]]の三女と結婚して婿養子に入る。土御門家も半家の家格であり、天文・暦道・陰陽道を家業としていた。晴雄は[[陰陽頭]]や[[民部卿]]などを務めていたが、[[明治維新]]を機に[[江戸幕府|幕府]]の[[天文方]]を廃止するよう主張し、[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]より許された。これにより、天文方の暦算や[[頒暦]]の権限は朝廷に返されることになり、朝廷で[[陰陽寮]]を統括していた土御門家が日本の編暦を一手に担うことになっていった。 === 陰陽師として === 1869年（明治2年）、養父・土御門晴雄が死去したことに伴い「土御門和丸ヨリ父晴雄死去届」『公文録』54巻。、晴栄が土御門家の家督を継いだ「土御門和丸ヨリ天文暦道ノ儀ニ付願」『公文録』55巻。。その翌年には[[天社禁止令]]により陰陽師の免許が禁止され、陰陽寮が廃止されることになったため、陰陽道を家業としていた土御門家にとっては苦難の時代が始まった。 陰陽寮が所管していた天文暦道は、[[大学校 (1869年)|大学校]]（[[東京大学|東京帝国大学]]の前身）の下に設置された天文暦道局に移管されることになったが、その際に土御門家の当主として晴栄は新政府より天文暦道御用掛を命じられた内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』16巻、内閣記録局、1889年、303頁。。しかし天文暦道局の本局が東京に移転されることになったため、京都の土御門家はその出先機関のような扱いとなり、出張所が設置されることになった「天文暦道局ヲ東京ニ移シ西京ハ土御門家ヲ以テ官庁ニ充ツ」『太政類典』118巻。。その後天文暦道局が星学局に改組されるにあたり、京都星学局出張所は廃止されることになり内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』2巻、内閣記録局、1889年、19頁。、晴栄も大学御用掛を解任されて内閣記録局編『法規分類大全』2巻、内閣記録局、1889年、20頁。「星学局出張所廃シニ付土御門和丸大学御用掛ヲ免セラル」『太政類典』117巻。、ここに土御門家は編暦に関する権限をすべて失うことになった。 === 政治家として === 明治維新とともに[[堂上家|堂上公家]]として[[華族]]に列していた晴栄は、1884年（明治17年）7月8日の[[華族令]]に基づき[[子爵]]に叙された[[小田部雄次]]『華族』[[中央公論新社]]、2006年、333頁。。その後1900年（明治33年）6月の貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙で当選して「帝國議會」『[[官報]]』5090號、[[印刷局]]、1900年6月22日、313面。同年6月21日から[[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院]]議員を務め『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、10頁。、1904年（明治37年）7月9日の任期満了まで在任{{efn|『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』48頁では任期満了前の6月に辞任としているが、帝国議会会議録では任期まで在任している。}}。1908年（明治41年）に行われた子爵議員の補選で再び当選、同年3月18日から貴族院議員を務め『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、17頁。、次の1911年（明治44年）7月の第4回子爵議員選挙でも再選されたが『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、20頁。、1915年（大正4年）10月16日議員在職のまま死去『貴族院要覧（丙）』昭和21年12月増訂、23頁。、同日付で[[正三位]]に叙された「叙任及辭令」『官報』965號、印刷局、1915年10月19日、411面。。 晴栄は晩年に子爵[[冷泉為柔]]の長女を後妻に迎えた。また、子爵[[松平信正 (丹波亀山藩主)|松平信正]]の次男・[[土御門晴行|晴行]]を自身の長女と結婚させて婿養子に迎えた。土御門家の家督と爵位はこの晴行が継いでいる。 == 略年譜 == * 1859年（安政6年）- 誕生 * 1870年（明治3年）- 天文暦道御用掛 * 1900年（明治33年）- 貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙当選 * 1908年（明治37年）- 貴族院子爵議員補欠選挙当選 * 1911年（明治44年）- 第四回貴族院子爵議員選挙当選 * 1915年（大正4年）- 死去 == 栄典 == * 1884年（明治17年）叙子爵 * [[1894年]]（明治27年）[[6月30日]] - [[従四位]]『官報』第3301号「叙任及辞令」1894年7月2日。 * 1915年（大正4年）- 贈正三位 == 家族・親族 == * [[土御門晴親]]（養祖父）- [[公家]]、[[陰陽師]] * [[錦織久隆]]（父）- 公家 * [[土御門晴雄]]（養父）- 公家、陰陽師 * [[冷泉為柔]]（岳父）- 公家、[[宮内省|宮内官僚]] * [[土御門藤子]]（養叔母）- 御所女中 * [[土御門晴善]]（養孫）- [[農学者]]、政治家 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|qid=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|qid=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|qid=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|qid=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|qq=|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|qq=|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

[[ja:土御門晴榮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[ja:]] '''Harenaga Tsuchimikado''' (土御門 晴栄, formerly written as 土御門 晴榮; June 30, 1859 – October 16, 1915) was a Japanese [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] and politician. He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|qq=|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|qq=|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|qq=|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.

The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. === Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

[[ja:土御門晴榮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[ja:]] '''Harenaga Tsuchimikado''' (土御門 晴栄, formerly written as 土御門 晴榮; June 30, 1859 – October 16, 1915) was a Japanese [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] and politician. He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.

== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

[[ja:土御門晴榮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[ja:]] '''Harenaga Tsuchimikado''' (土御門 晴栄, formerly written as 土御門 晴榮; June 30, 1859 – October 16, 1915) was a Japanese [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] and politician. He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

[[ja:土御門晴榮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[ja:]] '''Harenaga Tsuchimikado''' (土御門 晴栄, formerly written as 土御門 晴榮; June 30, 1859 – October 16, 1915) was a Japanese [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] and politician. He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17315120]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Harenaga [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) and served as a member of the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] and [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, [[:en:Astronomy|Astronomy]], and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]

[[ja:土御門晴榮]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[ja:]] '''Harenaga Tsuchimikado''' (土御門 晴栄, formerly written as 土御門 晴榮; June 30, 1859 – October 16, 1915) was a Japanese [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] and politician. He held the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount (Japan)]] and was posthumously awarded the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]], serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|onmyōdō]]. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new [[:en:Kazoku|kazoku]] system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}

== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Nishigori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Nishigori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the [[:en:House of Peers (Japan)|House of Peers]] in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Chamber of Peers|House of Peers (Japan)]]
