<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955|1=Politician}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928|1=member of the House of Peers}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478|1=Tsuchimikado clan}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550|1=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} (子爵) and served as a member of the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} and {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=Astronomy|1=Astronomy}}, and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Q11650550|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Chamber of Peers|en|Chamber of Peers|WD=Q2948408|lt=House of Peers (Japan)|1=Chamber of Peers}}
{{Japan-politician-stub}}

His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the {{ill|Chamber of Peers|en|Chamber of Peers|WD=Q2948408|lt=House of Peers (Japan)|1=Chamber of Peers}}, serving three terms.

His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.

In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=kazoku|1=Kazoku}} system. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門晴榮]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1859|6|30}}|death_date={{death date|1915|10|16}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|politician|WD=Q82955|1=Politician}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928|1=member of the House of Peers}}|family={{ill|Q11423478|WD=Q11423478|1=Tsuchimikado clan}}; {{ill|Q11650550|WD=Q11650550|1=Q11650550}}|native_name=土御門晴榮}} {{Infobox politician | name = Tsuchimikado Harenaga | native_name = 土御門 晴栄 | image = Harenaga Tsuchimikado.jpg | caption = Tsuchimikado Harenaga in 1913 | birth_date = {{Birth date|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Kyoto]], Japan | death_date = {{Death date and age|1915|10|16|1859|6|30|df=yes}} | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Onmyōji]], Politician | spouse = | children = [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]] | office = Member of the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} | term_start = June 21, 1900 | term_end = October 16, 1915 | party = Independent | Rank = {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} | awards = [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]] }}
'''Tsuchimikado Harenaga''' (土御門 晴栄, 1859–1915) was a Japanese [[onmyōji]] (master of yin-yang), scholar, and politician. He held the hereditary title of {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} (子爵) and served as a member of the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} and {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} periods.
== Overview == Harenaga was the head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado clan|qq=}}—a family historically associated with [[onmyōdō]] practices, {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=Astronomy|1=Astronomy}}, and [[Rekidō|Calendar-making]] (暦道). Despite being a direct descendant of the famed [[Abe no Seimei]], he faced numerous challenges after inheriting the clan during the societal transformations of the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The abolition of the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Onmyōryō]] (Bureau of Onmyōdō) marked the loss of the clan’s traditional privileges, including its authority over calendar-making.
Harenaga later became involved in politics, serving three terms in the House of Peers. His contributions as a public official earned him the posthumous honor of [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]].
== Early Life == Harenaga was born in 1859 as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a noble of the {{ill|Nishigori clan|ja|錦織家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori clan|1=Q11650550|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650550}}. Through maternal lines, he was connected to prominent ancestors in the Tsuchimikado and Nishigori families, who were noted for their service in onmyōdō and court positions.
In 1869, he was adopted by {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and became the head of the Tsuchimikado family following Haruo’s sudden death. Around this time, he was tasked by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} to oversee the remnants of onmyōdō practices. However, as the government centralized the calendar-making authority under a new [[Astronomical Bureau]], the Kyoto-based Tsuchimikado family lost its influence.
== Career == === Onmyōji and Astronomical Duties === In the early Meiji period, Harenaga was appointed as an official for astronomical and calendrical tasks within the newly formed {{ill|Astronomy Bureau|ja|天文暦道局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]]. However, after the Bureau's relocation to Tokyo, the Tsuchimikado family became a peripheral institution. Subsequent reorganizations eventually stripped the family of its remaining responsibilities in this field.
=== Political Career === In 1884, Harenaga was elevated to the Rank of viscount under the {{ill|Peerage Act (Japan)|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peerage Act (Japan)|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620084}}, becoming part of the newly established [[kazoku]] aristocracy. He entered the {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|en|House of Peers (Japan)|WD=Q715139|lt=House of Peers|1=House of Peers (Japan)}} in 1900, representing the viscounts. Serving three terms, he advocated for various reforms and remained active in the [[:en:Imperial Diet of Japan|Imperial Parliament]] until his death in 1915.
== Personal Life == Harenaga married the daughter of [[Reizei Tameyoshi]], further cementing his family’s noble ties. He adopted his son-in-law, [[Tsuchimikado Haruyuki]], to succeed him as the head of the Tsuchimikado family and as a viscount.
== Honors == * 1884: Elevated to {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount|1=Viscount}} (子爵) * 1894: Received the Rank of [[Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank]] * 1915: Posthumously awarded [[Third Rank|Senior Third Rank]]
== Legacy == Harenaga's efforts to preserve the heritage of the Tsuchimikado clan amidst political upheavals remain notable. Although the family lost its traditional authority in onmyōdō, its legacy continues through descendants and scholarly work. == See Also == * {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Chamber of Peers|en|Chamber of Peers|WD=Q2948408|lt=House of Peers (Japan)|1=Chamber of Peers}}

His childhood name was '''Kazumaru''' (和丸), and his birth surname was '''Nishigori''' (錦織). His name is sometimes read as "Hareyoshi."
He served as an official overseeing astronomy and the calendar, and later became a member of the {{ill|Chamber of Peers|en|Chamber of Peers|WD=Q2948408|lt=House of Peers (Japan)|1=Chamber of Peers}}, serving three terms.
== Biography == === Early Life === Born as the second son of {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}, a {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} noble, Harenaga was descended from the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}, known for their roles in {{ill|astronomy|ja|天文道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomy|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442664}}, {{ill|calendar making|ja|暦道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar making|1=Rekidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514958}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}. His ancestry traced back to prominent figures such as {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. He married into the Tsuchimikado family, becoming the adopted son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} and assuming the family headship after Haruo’s death in 1869.
=== As an Onmyōji === After succeeding as the head of the Tsuchimikado family, Harenaga faced difficulties due to the abolition of traditional institutions like the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. The government centralized calendrical functions under the Ministry of Education, diminishing the Tsuchimikado family's influence. Harenaga was briefly appointed as an official overseeing astronomical and calendrical affairs but lost this position when the government restructured these functions.
=== Political Career === Despite the decline of his family's traditional role, Harenaga remained active as a politician. In 1884, he was ennobled as a viscount under the new {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=kazoku|1=Kazoku}} system. He served three terms as a member of the House of Peers, representing the viscount class, until his death in 1915.
== Honors == * 1884: Created Viscount * 1894: Awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} * 1915: Posthumously awarded {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Nishigori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishigori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}} * Foster father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruo|ja|土御門晴雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruo|qq=}} * Foster grandson: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|ja|土御門晴善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyoshi|1=Hareyoshi Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17362688}}
